yesterdayStoryline.headline: Late levellers and early shocks reshape groups
yesterdayStoryline.subtitle: Qatar and Morocco held favourites, Scotland edged Haiti, and Australia punished Türkiye in Vancouver.

todayStoryline.headline: Curaçao debut frames Group E start
todayStoryline.subtitle: Germany open against the smallest World Cup qualifier before Group F tests depth and balance.

lines: Switzerland created heavily but failed to settle the match after Embolo's penalty.
Qatar's late point left Group B level after the opening round.
Morocco pressed through Brazil's midfield before Vinícius produced the equaliser against the run of play.
Brazil avoided defeat, but Ancelotti's side opened with clear structural concerns.
Scotland struck from a direct move and then survived Haiti's late pressure.
The win put Scotland above Brazil and Morocco in Group C.
Australia defended deep, rode Patrick Beach's saves, and punished Türkiye turnovers.
The Socceroos joined the United States on three points in Group D.

facts.home: Germany's 2006 World Cup summer later curdled into a bid-scandal probe around Franz Beckenbauer. Investigators could not rule out vote-buying, staining the old Sommermärchen glow deeply.
facts.away: Curaçao's 2026 qualification made a nation of about 180,000 smaller than many European cities. Dick Advocaat's return after family leave turned the debut into improbable Dutch-Caribbean theatre again.

facts.home: The Netherlands' 2010 final is still remembered for Nigel de Jong's chest-high kick on Xabi Alonso. Howard Webb later admitted it should have been red, not merely yellow.
facts.away: Japan sacked Vahid Halilhodzic in 2018 barely two months before Russia, despite qualification. He said he did not deserve it, then Akira Nishino took the job suddenly.

facts.home: Ivory Coast fired Vahid Halilhodzic in 2010 after an Africa Cup of Nations flop, despite World Cup qualification. Sven-Göran Eriksson then inherited Didier Drogba's golden generation cold overnight.
facts.away: Ecuador's 2022 place nearly unravelled when Chile challenged Byron Castillo's eligibility, alleging Colombian birth documents. FIFA kept Ecuador in, but CAS imposed a three-point 2026 qualifying deduction.

facts.home: Zlatan Ibrahimović hijacked Sweden's 2018 build-up by teasing a World Cup comeback from retirement. Captain Andreas Granqvist publicly told him to call Janne Andersson and stop the noise.
facts.away: Tunisia's 2023 football crisis peaked when federation president Wadie Jary was detained over alleged financial corruption. FIFA later installed a normalisation committee amid governance turmoil back in Tunis.

news.headline: UK says forces intercepted Russian shadow-fleet tanker
news.body: British Prime Minister Keir Starmer said he directed armed forces to intercept a shadow-fleet oil tanker attempting to pass through the English Channel. He framed the operation as a move against networks helping fund Russia's war in Ukraine.

footerNote: All times converted from official kickoffs
